steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The audience already knows what seedling emergence means, so no definition is needed. The task is to present the study's main finding — the negative effect of plant litter on seedling emergence — accurately and without padding it with irrelevant details.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The effect of plant litter, which included dead leaves and other plant matters, on seedling emergence has been the subject of scientist study.: Vague and never states the actual finding. ✗
  • Choice B — Wilby and Brown found that in a flooded grassland and a temperate midlatitude climate the presence of plant litter had a negative effect on seedling emergence.: Names the researchers, the key environmental conditions, and the core result — nothing superfluous. ✓
  • Choice C — In a 2001 study by Wilby and Brown, plant litter was found to have a negative effect on seedling emergence, which is when a seedling sprouts and begin photosynthesis.: Defines seedling emergence, which the audience already knows. The question explicitly says not to include this. ✗
  • Choice D — The findings of Wilby and Brown’s study were published in 2001.: States only the publication year — omits the actual finding entirely. ✗
That is the correct answer.
